name: Tiramisu (5)
recipeInstructions:
1. Arrange half the lady fingers in the bottom of a 9 x 13 pan.
2. Mix hot water with coffee granules and brandy. Pour half this mixture gently over lady fingers. Set aside.
3. Make zablione by beating egg yolks and sugar in the top of a double boiler until ivory colored. Add 1/4 cup brandy and whisk over simmering water until the mixture starts to thicken (8-10 minutes). Let cool slightly. Stir constantly while cooking.
4. Carefully mix zabliogne with mascarpone cheese substitute. It will appear quite liquid.
5. Whip egg whites until very stiff and fold into zabliogne/mascarpone mixture.
6. Pour half of zabliogne mixture over the lady fingers soaking in coffee/brandy mixture. Top with half whipped cream. Sprinkle with half cocoa powder and grated chocolate.
7. Place remaining lady fingers on top to create a second layer. Pour remaining coffee mixture carefully over these lady fingers. Repeat layers: zabliogne, whipped cream, cocoa powder, and grated chocolate.
8. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until set. Best if left overnight.
9. When ready to serve, decorate with piped-on whipped cream around the edges and more chocolate and/or cocoa powder if desired. Yields 10-12 servings depending on size of pieces. Rich in flavor, consider smaller pieces.
Note: Substitute Marsala wine for brandy if preferred. Mascarpone cheese substitute is made by blending 16 oz cream cheese, 1/3 cup sour cream, and 2 ounces whipping cream until light and fluffy, equivalent to about 2 1/2 cups of cheese. Use in equal amounts as mascarpone.
